Lineup classifier — training review

625 labeled frames · 77 misclassified · threshold 0.7 · trained 2026-07-24T01:10:09.247Z
dashed box = region of interest the classifier sees

accuracyprecisionrecall
train (522)0.8890.8830.876
test (103)0.8160.7350.857
How the classifier works

The model never sees the whole photo: the dashed region of interest is cropped, downscaled to 48×27 grayscale, and each of the 1296 pixels gets one learned weight (logistic regression). A pixel's brightness × its weight is its vote; the votes plus a bias are summed and squashed to a probability. ≥ 0.7 means “past crosswalk”.

the learned weight map — red pixels vote “past crosswalk” when bright, blue pixels vote “not yet” when bright

The explain button on any card shows this frame's actual model input and which pixels decided its outcome.

Why the model decided this

what the model saw (ROI → 48×27 grayscale)

learned weights (same for every frame)

this frame's votes (input × weight)

votes − weights — where this frame falls short of a fully-bright ROI: strongest where an influential pixel is dark

red pushes toward “past crosswalk”, blue toward “not yet”; stronger color = stronger pull.
